name: Pla Preeo Wan (Sweet and Sour Flounder)
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Diced, peeled and seeded cucumber
2 cups Diced, cored and seeded ripe tomatoes
1/2 cup Coarsely chopped fresh cilantro leaves
recipeInstructions:
1. Carefully remove any tiny bones from the flounder using a tweezer. Place the fish on a broiler tray lined with aluminum foil and preheat the broiler.
2. Combine diced pineapple, cucumber, tomatoes, onions, and pineapple juice in a heavy saucepan. Simmer over medium-low heat for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
3. Reduce heat to low, add garlic and ginger, season with salt, and continue cooking for another 5 minutes.
4. Remove 1/4 cup of the liquid from the saucepan and mix it with cornstarch until smooth. Stir this mixture back into the saucepan and cook, stirring, for an additional 2 minutes to thicken the fruit sauce slightly. Remove from heat and set aside.
5. Brush the fish fillets with olive oil and drizzle with lime juice. Season with salt. Place the fish about 4 inches from the heat source and broil for 4-5 minutes, or until opaque and flakes easily. Remove, cut in half crosswise, and place one piece of fish in each of 8 shallow bowls.
6. Heat the fruit sauce over medium heat for 1 minute, stirring in cilantro. Ladle evenly over the fish. Serve immediately.
Per serving: 212 calories, 5 grams fat, 60 milligrams cholesterol.
